e2e.label: Playwright smoke gate
e2e.note: [e2e-unverified] Seed-script RLS blocker is FIXED (prisma/seed.ts now wraps every write to an RLS-protected table in a transaction that sets app.current_tenant_id via SET LOCAL — verified end-to-end, the seed run completes and the admin user row genuinely exists). But E2E is still blocked by a DEEPER, previously-undiscovered P0 finding: the running app never sets app.current_tenant_id anywhere in its request pipeline (grepped the whole app source — zero occurrences), so PostgreSQL's FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on users/invoices/payments/employees/patients/payroll_runs/journals/customers/vendors/sales_orders/purchase_orders/audit_logs makes those tables invisible to any DB role without BYPASSRLS — confirmed directly via psql: `SELECT count(*) FROM users` returns 0 without the session var set, 1 with it set. This is why POST /auth/login 401s even though the seeded admin row is genuinely in the table. The app's actual tenant isolation lives in a separate mechanism (packages/database/src/index.ts's Prisma $extends hook + applyTenantScope, AsyncLocalStorage-based) that the RLS migration (20260626120000_rls_policies) was never wired into — the two tenant-isolation layers are disconnected. Declined to fix this broadly (wiring SET LOCAL into the shared Prisma extension touches every module using those 12 tables and needs dedicated regression testing, not a side-fix during an Inventory-focused session) or to bypass RLS. Documented in CHANGELOG/MODULE_REGISTRY as a P0 cross-cutting finding for a dedicated session.
